rules.description: All releases MUST follow the v1.2.3.4 format (major.minor.patch.build).
Model changes MUST increment major version and reset all lower segments to 0.
Service changes MUST increment minor version and reset patch and build to 0.
Bug fixes or configuration changes MUST increment patch version and reset build to 0.
Build/pipeline changes MUST increment build version only.
When multiple changes occur, the highest-order change determines version increment (model > service > patch > build).
Default version is V0 — version is inferred and MUST NOT be included in the filename.
Model changes MUST require creation of a new Vn folder (e.g., V1, V2).
Model-driven service changes MUST create a new service file in the model's Vn folder (e.g., Services/.../V{n}/{Entity}V{n}Service.cs).
Previous version files MUST NOT be overwritten — always create new versioned folders.
File versioning MUST be applied to maintain backward compatibility.
Service behavior-only changes (model still at V0) MUST produce a file named {Entity}ServiceV{n}.cs at the service location root — no version folder is created.
When a versioned model's service behavior changes, the service MUST be named {Entity}V{m}ServiceV{n}.cs inside the model's V{m} folder.

When model and service behavior change together in the same release, the behavior version MUST be absent from the file name — only the model version increments and the behavior version is implied as V0.
V0 APIs have no version prefix in the route.
Model version changes MUST use Vn prefix in routes (e.g., /V1/students).
Service version changes MUST use Vn.m prefix in routes (e.g., /V1.1/students).
API routes are immutable — existing routes MUST NOT be modified or removed.
API versioning MUST reflect the underlying model or service version.
Deprecated APIs MUST include sunset metadata indicating when they will be removed.
Deprecated code MUST include warning messages to notify consumers.
Deprecated code MUST use the [Obsolete] attribute in C# or equivalent in other languages.
Deprecation period MUST provide adequate time for consumers to migrate (recommended minimum: 6 months).
Deprecation notices MUST be communicated through documentation, API responses, and release notes.
